name: IIIF
comment:
Give a URL to a IIIF Resource, fetch, and decode the result.

If you know that the URL will respond with a Manifest, then you can call `requestManifest`. Likewise, if you
know that the response will be an Image API info.json response, call `requestInfo`.

However, if you are unsure if the response will be a manifest, collection, canvas, or range (as you might be when traversing
a IIIF collection that has embedded collections or manifests) then call `requestResource`. This also supports dereferencing
canvas and range URIs.

Additionally, the JSON-LD context strings are provided for both v2 and v3 Image and Presentation APIs. These are used
internally to determine how to decode the response, but they may also be useful in consuming applications; for example, if
a server uses content negotiation to switch between a v2 or v3 API response, then you can use this string as the `profile=`
value. (See, for example: <https://iiif.io/api/image/3.0/#51-image-information-request>)

@docs requestManifest, requestInfo, requestResource
@docs v2PresentationContext, v3PresentationContext, v2ImageContext, v3ImageContext

values.name: requestInfo
values.comment: Request a IIIF info.json response by URL.
values.type: (Result.Result Http.Error IIIF.ImageInfo.IIIFInfo -> msg) -> List.List String.String -> String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: requestManifest
values.comment: Request a manifest by URL.
values.type: (Result.Result Http.Error IIIF.Presentation.IIIFManifest -> msg) -> List.List String.String -> String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: requestResource
values.comment: Request a IIIF resource (manifest or collection) by URL.
values.type: (Result.Result Http.Error IIIF.Presentation.IIIFResource -> msg) -> List.List String.String -> String.String -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: v2ImageContext
values.comment: The IIIF v2 Image Info Context String. Useful for detecting a IIIF v2 Image Server Response

values.name: v2PresentationContext
values.comment: The IIIF v2 Presentation Context String. Useful for detecting a IIIF v2 Manifest

values.name: v3ImageContext
values.comment: The IIIF v3 Image Info Context String. Useful for detecting a IIIF v3 Image Server Response

values.name: v3PresentationContext
values.comment: The IIIF v3 Presentation Context String. Useful for detecting a IIIF v3 Manifest

name: IIIF.Image

unions.name: ImageFormat
unions.comment: The format portion of an image request.

unions.name: ImageQuality
unions.comment: The quality portion of an image request.

unions.name: ImageRegion
unions.comment:
The region portion of an image request.

Example:

    SquareRegion
cases: { x : Basics.Int, y : Basics.Int, w : Basics.Int, h : Basics.Int }
{ x : Basics.Float, y : Basics.Float, w : Basics.Float, h : Basics.Float }

unions.name: ImageRotation
unions.comment: The rotation portion of an image request.

unions.name: ImageSize
unions.comment:
The parameters allowed, in order, are

    <max>,
    <^max>,
    <w,>,
    <^w,>,
    <,h>,
    <^,h>,
    <pct:n>,
    <^pct:n>,
    <w,h>,
    <^w,h>,
    <!w,h>,
    <^!w,h>

For IIIF v2 the keyword <full> will be mapped to the <max> type. Since the
'full' value was marked as deprecated in IIIF 2.1, and the <max> type was available
then, any conversions back to IIIF v2 image URI will use the "max" string.

unions.name: ImageUri
unions.comment:
A parsed IIIF Image URI. The InfoUri is used to construct URLs to the 'info.json' document,
while the ImageUri is used to construct Image API calls for regions, sizes, etc.

Example:

    imageUriToInfoUri (InfoUri server)

aliases.name: ImageRequestParameters
aliases.comment:
Parameters for a full IIIF image request URI.

Example:

    { host = "https://example.org"
    , prefix = "iiif/2/abc123"
    , region = FullRegion
    , size = MaxSize
    , rotation = NormalRotation 0
    , quality = DefaultQuality
    , format = JpegFormat
    }
aliases.type: { host : String.String, prefix : String.String, region : IIIF.Image.ImageRegion, size : IIIF.Image.ImageSize, rotation : IIIF.Image.ImageRotation, quality : IIIF.Image.ImageQuality, format : IIIF.Image.ImageFormat }

aliases.name: ImageServerParameters
aliases.comment: Parameters for the base image server URI (info.json).
aliases.type: { host : String.String, prefix : String.String }

values.name: createFormatComponent
values.comment: Render the format component for an image request.

values.name: createImageAddress
values.comment: Render a IIIFImageUri as a string URL.

values.name: createImageUri
values.comment: Render a full image request URI as a URL string.

values.name: createInfoUri
values.comment: Render a base image server URI as an `info.json` URL.

values.name: createQualityComponent
values.comment: Render the quality component for an image request.

values.name: createRegionComponent
values.comment: Render the region component for an image request.

values.name: createRotationComponent
values.comment: Render the rotation component for an image request.

values.name: createSizeComponent
values.comment: Render the size component for an image request.

values.name: exactHeightOnlySizeParser
values.comment: Parse an exact height-only size (`^,h`).

values.name: exactPercentSizeParser
values.comment: Parse an exact percent size (`^pct:n`).

values.name: exactScaledWidthAndHeightSizeParser
values.comment: Parse an exact scaled width and height size (`^!w,h`).

values.name: exactWidthAndHeightSizeParser
values.comment: Parse an exact width and height size (`^w,h`).

values.name: exactWidthOnlySizeParser
values.comment: Parse an exact width-only size (`^w,`).

values.name: formatParser
values.comment: Parse the format from a `<quality>.<format>` string.

values.name: fullSquareRegionParser
values.comment: Parse the `full` or `square` region variants.

values.name: heightOnlySizeParser
values.comment: Parse a height-only size (`,h`).

values.name: imageRegionParser
values.comment: Parse the region component of an image request.

values.name: imageServerToImageRequest
values.comment: Expand a base image server URI into default image request parameters.

values.name: imageSizeParser
values.comment: Parse the size component of an image request.

values.name: imageUriFromComponents
values.comment:
Parse a split path into an image request URI. Like infoUriParser, this
is useful if you have a server and a list of path components and want
an ImageUri record.


    img =
        imageUriParser "https://example.org"
            [ "some", "prefix", "full", "100,200", "20", "default.jpg" ]

    -- Just (ImageUri
    -- { host = "https://example.org"
    -- , prefix = "some/prefix"
    --, region = FullRegion
    --, size = WidthAndHeightSize ( 100, 200 )
    --, rotation = NormalRotation 20
    --, quality = DefaultQuality
    --, format = JpegFormat
    --- })

values.name: imageUriToInfoUri
values.comment: Convert a full image request URI to its info.json URI.

values.name: infoUriFromComponents
values.comment:
Construct a split path into an info.json URI. Useful if you have
a server and a list of prefix path components and you want to generate
an InfoUri record. Will remove any 'info.json' components that already exist.


    info =
        infoUriParser "https://example.org" [ "some", "prefix", "info.json" ]

    -- Just (InfoUri { host = "https://example.org", prefix = "some/prefix"]

values.name: infoUriToImageUri
values.comment:
Convert an URL for info.json to a image request URI, using
a default set of arguments.

values.name: maxSizeParser
values.comment: Parse the `max`/`full` size variants.

values.name: mirroredRotationParser
values.comment: Parse a mirrored rotation value (`!` prefix).

values.name: normalRotationParser
values.comment: Parse a non-mirrored rotation value.

values.name: parseImageAddress
values.comment: Parse a IIIF Image URL string into a IIIFImageUri record.

values.name: parseImageUrl
values.comment: Parse an `Url` into a IIIF image or info.json URI.

values.name: pctSizeRegionParser
values.comment: Parse a region expressed as `pct:x,y,w,h`.

values.name: percentSizeParser
values.comment: Parse a percent size (`pct:n`).

values.name: qualityParser
values.comment: Parse the quality from a `<quality>.<format>` string.

values.name: rotationParser
values.comment: Parse a rotation component (normal or mirrored).

values.name: scaledWidthAndHeightSizeParser
values.comment: Parse a scaled width and height size (`!w,h`).

values.name: setImageUriRegion
values.comment:
Set the region component of an IIIFImageUri. If a InfoUri is provided
it is converted to a ImageUri and then the region is set.

values.name: setImageUriSize
values.comment:
Set the size component of an image URI. If an InfoUri is provided
it will convert it to an ImageUri and then apply the size setting.

values.name: sizeRegionParser
values.comment: Parse a region expressed as `x,y,w,h`.

values.name: thumbnailUrlFromInfo
values.comment:
Parses an info.json URL and constructs an Image API Url that
returns an image that is 180px wide, suitable for showing a thumbnail image.

That is, it will take a URL like: `https://example.org/some/prefix/info.json` and
will return a URL like: `https://example.org/some/prefix/full/180,/0/default.jpg`

values.name: whSizeParser
values.comment: Parse a `w,h` pair into a tuple.

values.name: widthAndHeightSizeParser
values.comment: Parse a width and height size (`w,h`).

values.name: widthOnlySizeParser
values.comment: Parse a width-only size (`w,`).

name: IIIF.ImageInfo
comment:
Types and helpers for IIIF Image API `info.json`.

@docs IIIFInfo, InfoJson, InfoProfile, WidthHeight, WidthHeightScale, toInfoJson

unions.name: IIIFInfo
unions.comment: A version-tagged info.json wrapper.

aliases.name: InfoJson
aliases.type: { id : IIIF.Image.ImageUri, width : Basics.Int, height : Basics.Int, sizes : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.ImageInfo.WidthHeight), tiles : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.ImageInfo.WidthHeightScale) }

aliases.name: InfoProfile
aliases.comment: Optional profile metadata from the Image API.
aliases.type: { formats : Maybe.Maybe (List.List String.String), qualities : Maybe.Maybe (List.List String.String), supports : Maybe.Maybe (List.List String.String), maxWidth : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, maxHeight : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int }

aliases.name: WidthHeight
aliases.type: { width : Basics.Int, height : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: WidthHeightScale
aliases.type: { width : Basics.Int, height : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, scaleFactors : List.List Basics.Int }

values.name: toInfoJson
values.comment: Extract the underlying info.json record.

name: IIIF.Language

unions.name: Language
unions.comment:
The encoded language. This can be helpful to try and map
a user's locale to a decoded language map.

If the language map has localizations, e.g., "en" or "de", then
it will be decoded as `LanguageCode "en"` or `LanguageCode "de"`.

If it has the special case value of "none" (used for non-localizable
strings in a language map) then it will be decoded as `None`.

If there is no language map (common with v2 manifests) then it will
be decoded as `Default`.

unions.name: LanguageValues
unions.comment: One language tag paired with its values.

aliases.name: LabelValue
aliases.comment:
A label/value pair, each expressed as a language map.

Example:

    { label = [ LanguageValues English [ "Title" ] ]
    , value = [ LanguageValues English [ "Some description" ] ]
    }
aliases.type: { label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, value : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap }

aliases.name: LanguageMap
aliases.comment: A language map holds one or more language-tagged values.
aliases.type: List.List IIIF.Language.LanguageValues

values.name: extractLabelFromLanguageMap
values.comment:
Extract a string from a language map given a language.

Use this function to present users with a localized view of the values in a manifest.

A language can be detected from the user's browser settings or a preference. Using this
detected value, and a LanguageMap object, this function will:

  - Choose the language string that matches their language; if none then
  - Choose the language string that matches anything marked with 'none'; if none then
  - Choose the first language string that matches the 'Default', which is likely the first language.
  - Choose the language string that matches the 'English', which is the most likely to occur
    in the widest number of manifests.

If none of those work, then it's likely the LanguageMap is somehow broken. This function will finally fall
back to returning "[No language value found]" as the string.

The 'value' of a LanguageMap is always a list. This function will present multiple values in the list
with a semicolon separator.

values.name: labelValueDecoder
values.comment: Decoder for label/value pairs (v3 style).

values.name: languageMapLabelDecoder
values.comment: Decoder for a language map object.

values.name: stringToLanguageMapLabelDecoder
values.comment:
Decoder for the case where an expected language map
is encoded as a string.

values.name: v2LabelValueDecoder
values.comment: Decoder for label/value pairs (v2 style).

values.name: v2LanguageMapLabelDecoder
values.comment: Decoder for v2 language map variants.

name: IIIF.Presentation

unions.name: Behavior
unions.comment: Manifest Behavior hints (IIIF v3; See ViewingHint for v2).

unions.name: CollectionItem
unions.comment:
A collection item (either a collection or manifest). This is used to represent
the two possible content items in a IIIF Collection.

unions.name: IIIFCanvas
unions.comment: A version-tagged IIIF canvas wrapper.

unions.name: IIIFCollection
unions.comment: A version-tagged IIIF collection wrapper.

unions.name: IIIFManifest
unions.comment: A version-tagged IIIF manifest wrapper.

unions.name: IIIFRange
unions.comment: A version-tagged IIIF range wrapper.

unions.name: IIIFResource
unions.comment:
A top-level IIIF resource. Useful for when you are requesting
a IIIF resource but you don't know whether you will get a manifest
or collection as a result.

unions.name: ImageType
unions.comment: Image type values for a canvas image.

unions.name: MediaFormats
unions.comment: Supported media format identifiers.

unions.name: RangeItem
unions.comment: A range item (range or canvas).

unions.name: ResourceTypes
unions.comment: Resource type identifiers.

unions.name: ServiceTypes
unions.comment: Service type identifiers.

unions.name: ViewingDirection
unions.comment: Viewing direction for a manifest.

unions.name: ViewingHint
unions.comment: Viewing hint values (IIIF v2; See Behavior for v3).

unions.name: ViewingLayout
unions.comment: Viewing layout values. Uses different types for the V2 and V3 APIs.

aliases.name: Canvas
aliases.comment: A IIIF canvas record (v2 and v3).
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : Maybe.Maybe IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, width : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, height : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, images : List.List IIIF.Presentation.Image, viewingLayout : Maybe.Maybe IIIF.Presentation.ViewingLayout }

aliases.name: Collection
aliases.comment: The core collection record (v2 and v3).
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, summary : Maybe.Maybe IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, items : List.List IIIF.Presentation.CollectionItem }

aliases.name: HomePage
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, format : IIIF.Presentation.MediaFormats, type_ : IIIF.Presentation.ResourceTypes }

aliases.name: Image
aliases.type: { id : IIIF.Image.ImageUri, label : Maybe.Maybe IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, imageType : IIIF.Presentation.ImageType, service : List.List IIIF.Presentation.ServiceTypes }

aliases.name: Logo
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, format : IIIF.Presentation.MediaFormats, type_ : IIIF.Presentation.ResourceTypes, width : Basics.Int, height : Basics.Int, service : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.Presentation.ServiceObject) }

aliases.name: Manifest
aliases.comment:
The core manifest record. This record type normalizes the differences between
v2 and v3 manifests, making them both fit the same structure. The differences are
normalized in the decoding process.
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, metadata : List.List IIIF.Language.LabelValue, viewingDirection : IIIF.Presentation.ViewingDirection, summary : Maybe.Maybe IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, viewingLayout : IIIF.Presentation.ViewingLayout, canvases : List.List IIIF.Presentation.Canvas, ranges : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.Presentation.Range), homepage : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.Presentation.HomePage), logo : Maybe.Maybe IIIF.Presentation.Image, provider : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.Presentation.Provider), thumbnail : Maybe.Maybe IIIF.Presentation.Image, requiredStatement : Maybe.Maybe IIIF.Presentation.RequiredStatement }

aliases.name: PaintedMedia
aliases.comment: A minimal painted media reference (used for canvas annotation bodies).
aliases.type: { id : String.String }

aliases.name: Provider
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, type_ : IIIF.Presentation.ResourceTypes, homepage : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.Presentation.HomePage), logo : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.Presentation.Logo), seeAlso : Maybe.Maybe (List.List IIIF.Presentation.SeeAlso) }

aliases.name: Range
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, items : List.List IIIF.Presentation.RangeItem, metadata : List.List IIIF.Language.LabelValue }

aliases.name: RequiredStatement
aliases.type: { label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, value : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap }

aliases.name: SeeAlso
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, format : IIIF.Presentation.MediaFormats, type_ : IIIF.Presentation.ResourceTypes }

aliases.name: Service
aliases.type: { id : String.String, type_ : IIIF.Presentation.ServiceTypes, profile : String.String }

aliases.name: ServiceObject
aliases.comment: A service record attached to another resource.
aliases.type: { id : String.String, serviceType : IIIF.Presentation.ServiceTypes }

aliases.name: Thumbnail
aliases.type: { id : String.String, label : IIIF.Language.LanguageMap, format : IIIF.Presentation.MediaFormats, type_ : IIIF.Presentation.ResourceTypes, mediaServices : List.List IIIF.Presentation.Service }

values.name: canvasAspect
values.comment:
Compute the aspect ratio for a canvas.

If (for whatever reason) the width and height of a canvas is not
available, or contains invalid values (e.g., "0") then this will
return 1.

values.name: canvasLabel
values.comment: Read a canvas label as a string (default language).

values.name: isPagedLayout
values.comment: True when the layout represents a paged/spread view.

values.name: manifestViewingLayout
values.comment: Read the manifest viewing layout.

values.name: mediaFormatFromString
values.comment: Convert a string to a media format identifier.

values.name: primaryImageUriForCanvas
values.comment:
Pick the primary image URI for a canvas (if available).

On canvases with only one image, it will return the URI of the first; on canvases
with multiple images it will return the URI of the Primary image, or, if no images
are marked as the primary one, it will return the first in the list.

If the list of images on the canvas is empty, it will return Nothing.

values.name: resourceTypeFromString
values.comment: Convert a string to a resource type identifier.

values.name: stringToBehavior
values.comment: Convert a string to a behavior value.

values.name: stringToServiceType
values.comment: Convert a string to a service type.

values.name: stringToViewingDirection
values.comment: Convert a string to a viewing direction value.

values.name: stringToViewingHint
values.comment: Convert a string to a viewing hint value.

values.name: toCanvases
values.comment: Read the manifest canvases.

values.name: toHomepage
values.comment: Read the manifest homepage links.

values.name: toLabel
values.comment: Read the manifest label.

values.name: toLogo
values.comment: Read the manifest logo image.

values.name: toManifest
values.comment: Unwrap a version-tagged manifest.

values.name: toMetadata
values.comment: Read the manifest metadata list.

values.name: toProvider
values.comment: Read the manifest provider list.

values.name: toRanges
values.comment: Read the manifest ranges (if present).

values.name: toRequiredStatement
values.comment: Read the manifest required statement (if present).

values.name: toSummary
values.comment: Read the manifest summary.

values.name: toViewingDirection
values.comment: Read the manifest viewing direction.

values.name: withManifest
values.comment: Apply an accessor to the underlying manifest.

name: IIIF.Version
comment:
The IIIF API version in use.

Example:

    case version of
        IIIFV2 ->
            "IIIF v2"

        IIIFV3 ->
            "IIIF v3"

@docs IIIFVersion

unions.name: IIIFVersion
unions.comment: IIIF API version tag.
